text: Barely. Real Mathlib proofs are overwhelmingly flat: of 1,715 theorems only ~4% are decomposable (>=2 in-proof nodes), with max dependency-poset depth 3. Extrapolated to ~8k decomposable declarations over full Mathlib - marginally clearing the 5-10k threshold. The granularity axis exists but its thin supply is the project's biggest live risk.
